question:
Will RCD Mallorca win on 2025-10-29?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for October 29, 2025
If RCD Mallorca wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.rfef.es/

evidences.title: Mallorca suffer major injury setback for Barcelona La Liga ...
evidences.content: ## Costa misses Barca game ahead of Premier League switch Mallorca’s preparations have been hit by issues focused on midfielder Samu Costa, who according to Diario AS, will miss the gam due to injury. A knee issue will keep him sidelined with Omar Mascarell also absent due to a suspension carried over from last season. Costa’s absence could also mean the end of his career in Mallorca, with his agent Jorge Mendes requesting talks with the club hierarchy over an exit. The Portugal international has attracted interest from the Premier League this month and he is pushing to move on before the window closes. Bournemouth are rumoured to be leading the chase as Costa looks to keep himself in contention for a place in Portugal’s FIFA 2026 World Cup qualification plans for September. evidences.title: Real Madrid squad concerns ahead of Mallorca match
evidences.content: Spanish football federation rules require a team to have at least seven players registered for the relevant category on the field at all times, or the referee could suspend the game. Dani Carvajal, Eder Militao, David Alaba, Antonio Rudiger, Ferland Mendy, Eduardo Camavinga, Lucas Vazquez, Vinicius Junior and Rodrygo were listed as injured, while Aurelien Tchouameni was unavailable through suspension.

evidences.title: Así es LaLiga 2025/26 para el RCD Mallorca
evidences.content: FIRST TEAM The LaLiga 2025/26 calendar is announced Our campaign will begin against the champions FC Barcelona The LaLiga schedule for the 2025/26 season. With this being our fifth consecutive season at the highest levels of Spanish football, the opening fixture will be at the Estadi Mallorca Son Moix against reigning champions FC Barcelona on the weekend of 17 August. The second matchday will also be at home and the rival will be RC Celta de Vigo and the first away game will come a week later at Real Madrid CF. The year 2025 will close at the Mestalla against Valencia CF while another notable date for the diary is our 110th anniversary where we will face CA Osasuna in Navarre. The Estadi Mallorca Son Moix will bid farewell to the season against Real Oviedo on the final matchday. evidences.title: RCD Mallorca Tickets — La Liga 2026/2027
evidences.content: Book early for marquee fixtures. Derbies, top-six clashes, and Champions League nights see prices rise 30-60% in the final fortnight before kick-off. Compare seat positions, not just prices. A £15 difference between rows usually buys a 30+ metre improvement in sightline. View the seat map before you commit. Check away allocations as well. Away ends are sometimes priced below home equivalents and the atmosphere is often better. Mid-week is cheaper than weekend. Tuesday and Wednesday RCD Mallorca fixtures consistently list 25-40% below comparable Saturday games. Late drops happen. 48-72 hours before kick-off, sellers with unused tickets often discount 15-30% to clear inventory. [...] For routine mid-week fixtures, listings often drop in the final 48-72 hours as sellers clear unused inventory. Set up a watch on the fixture page and you'll get a notification the moment a price drops on the seat band you've chosen. evidences.title: RCD Mallorca launches the Torneo de Empresas 2025 | Web Oficial
evidences.content: RCD Mallorca announced the Torneo de Empresas 2025, with teams guaranteed at least three 20-minute matches and up to six over the weekend. The schedule lists group-stage play on Saturday, June 14, followed by quarterfinals, semifinals, the final, an award ceremony, and celebration on Sunday, June 15. The club says the tournament will be supported by its event services.

evidences.title: RCD Mallorca’s return to competitive football ends in 4-0 defeat against FC Barcelona | RCD Mallorca | Web Oficial
evidences.content: Arturo Vidal, Martin Braithwaite, Jordi Alba and Lionel Messi hit the target as our first game back ends in a loss following the domestic season's suspension Our first game back after 102 days since our last competitive fixture was unfortunate to end in a 4-0 defeat against league leaders FC Barcelona. Ahead of our fight for survival upon confirmation the domestic season would resume following the COVID-19 pandemic, #HastaLaUltimaGota, translated as ‘#UntilTheLastDrop’, has been the club’s choice mantra, and despite the margin of the loss, it was a sentiment personified by the valiant performance of a team adjusting to ‘the new normal’ of football. [...] With chances few and far between for the remainder of the second half, Jordi Alba put the game beyond any potential comeback with 10 minutes to go, racing in-behind to slot the ball past the advancing Manolo Reina, before Lionel Messi then added a fourth from inside the box with a composed finish. We will quickly have to recover from the setback as we travel to Estadio de la Cerámica for a clash with Villarreal in less than 72 hours' time.
